evidence_excerpt: Postoperative course included ventriculoperitoneal shunt for posthemorrhagic hydrocephalus, but follow-up was uneventful and he was discharged with full neurologic recovery; ventricular dilatation resolved by six-month follow-up.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Follow-up was uneventful and control CT at six months showed resolution of ventricular dilatation.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: During postoperative treatment in the ICU for 40 days, ventriculoperitoneal shunt was performed due to posthemorrhagic hydrocephalus.
Ventriculoperitoneal shunt was performed due to posthemorrhagic hydrocephalus during the postoperative ICU course.
Patient’s follow-up was uneventful and he was discharged with full neurologic recovery.
Resolution of the ventricular dilatation was seen in the control computed tomography in his sixth month follow up.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact dates are not provided; index time approximated to the CT angiography confirming LPChA aneurysm prior to emergent embolization. Long-term status of the VP shunt is not specified; partial_recovery chosen due to ongoing device placement despite full neurologic recovery.
